body.title: Slate sections: salience-ordered (Next up → Blocked → After that → Decided → Done)
body.result: Reorganized daily slate to salience-first sections (Next up → Blocked → After that → Decided → Done → Ad hoc → Slate summary). Updated context-load template, cc_queue.py append target, wrote one-shot reorder_slate_sections.py, migrated 17/18 existing slate files, re-ingested all 18 into clan.memories. Tests pass. D-slate-salience-order-2026-04-20 complete.
body.description:
Current slate has Planned/Ad hoc/Done today ordering — most recent work is at the bottom and next work is buried. Reorganize to salience-first so session-start readers can stop reading once they have enough context.

## New section order
1. ## Next up — 1-3 tickets to work on right now
2. ## Blocked — stuck items (an unblock event promotes to Next up)
3. ## After that — rest of the queue
4. ## Decided — decisions filed this session
5. ## Done — shipped today

## Changes
1. context-load skill SKILL.md step 1 heredoc: update template to new section order
2. cc_queue.py _append_to_todays_slate: target '## Done' instead of '## Done today'
3. One-time migration script: reformat existing 18 slate files (Done today → Done, Planned — next up → Next up, Planned — remaining → After that, Planned → After that, Slate summary appended to Done)

## Not in scope
- Changing how the palace slate rows store sections (migrate_slates_to_palace.py parses any ## header, no change needed)
